title: Real customer data in prompts
description: A bug report, fixture, or AI prompt includes raw personal data from production or support records.
body: Failure mode: real records are copied into model context, generated tests, screenshots, or issue descriptions. This creates new uncontrolled sinks and retention. Detection signals include names, emails, health/payment fields, screenshots, or raw payloads in prompts and artifacts. Remediation is redaction or synthetic reproduction. Human gate is required for any real regulated data already shared. Use when a proposed approach skips personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion.
